free_text: Simple Nuclear Reactions of Indium with 30- and 2.9-
GeV Protons
The irradiations at 30 GeV were
performed in the circulating beam of the Brookhaven
Alternating Gradient Synchrotron.
The irradiations at 2.9 GeV were performed in the
circulating beam of the Cosmotron.
The gamma-ray spectra were usually
measured with the scintillation counter subtending a
large solid angle.
The activity measurements were performed with a
calibrated 3-in. by 3-in. NaI crystal connected to a
256 channel pulse-height analyzer as well as with
beta-proportional counters.
The activity of Na24 in the monitor foil was determined
by assay of the foil with a calibrated betaproportional
counter.
Individual cross sections have an
error of 5-20% due to uncertainties in the decay
schemes and to systematic errors in analysis of gamma
spectra.
Table II
** By authors ** In most cases, there were no
systematic differences between results of thin and
thick target experiments.

OS. ERR-ANALYS modified;

Thin targets consisted of high-purity (99.99%) indium
metal evaporated to 4 mg/cm2 thickness onto high-
purity 0.001-in. aluminum. Target stack also included
a 0.001-in. aluminum monitor and several thin aluminum
foils. Over-all target thickness about 20-25 mg/cm2.
